messages.content: Please edit this text
I'm sorry, but I cannot edit the text as you have not provided any text to edit. Please provide the text that needs editing and I will be happy to assist you.

I am a general dentist and I am going to pursue a master's degree in public health with an emphasis on leadership in practice at the University of North Carolina Chapel Hill.

I think I could not present the relationship between my current degree and the program I want to pursue on my last embassy appointment. Now I feel confident to elaborate on that.

First I would like to mention that normally in my country students go straight to the university after high school. I mean my Doctor of Dental Surgery does not mean that I first pursue a bachelor then a master and so on. So pursuing a Master's degree in public health does not mean that I am pursuing a lower-level degree compared to mine.  

I have been working as a general dentist. I come up with the idea of opening my own dental clinic. After two years of clinical work gaining first-hand experience in a healthcare environment and knowing business fundamentals by being in a startup consultancy company, I found out that managing and running a health-related company/clinic is so complicated. For example, it is inappropriate to use business strategies in a healthcare system because of morality. As a result, I have to gain new skills in a relevant environment to be able to design and run a sustainable system that is based on morality and profitable. Also, managing communication between different sectors such as nurses, doctors, and patients, and working different wards in harmony needs knowledge and skills rather than just academic texts. And this program will provide me with the chance to practicum of 200 hours of training in an actual setting. Also, the healthcare system in the US is much more similar to Iran than any other country. Unfortunately, this program in Iran is just focused on research and it doesn't have concentrations so I cannot study specifically what I am looking for needed in my future career.

Also, I am grateful for scholarships and a wonderful job offer that helps me to open my own dental ward as well as work as a clinical leader with a good salary. Starting my ward in a well-known surgical clinic would help me to introduce dentistry under sedation more to the community and it would help people to overcome their phobia of dental treatment and come to the clinic with peace of mind. So I would take steps to help my people to increase their oral hygiene and make life better for them.  Besides, I will learn how to design the clinic system to be both affordable for patients and profitable for the system. As a result, we will be able to serve the disadvantaged as well.

As a general dentist, I have gained experience in a healthcare environment over the past two years and developed an interest in opening my own dental clinic. However, I quickly realized that running a healthcare system requires a different set of skills than just clinical expertise. I need to be able to design and manage a sustainable system that is both profitable and moral since it is inappropriate to use business strategies in a healthcare system. In addition, communication and coordination between different sectors, including nurses, doctors, and patients, are essential components of a successful healthcare system.

Therefore, I have decided to pursue a Master's degree in public health with an emphasis on leadership in practice at the University of North Carolina Chapel Hill. This program will provide me with the skills and knowledge required to design and implement a sustainable healthcare system that is both profitable and compliant with morality. Furthermore, I will have the opportunity to undergo 200 hours of training in an actual setting, which will help me apply my knowledge in a real-world environment.

It is important to note that pursuing a Master's degree in public health does not mean that I am pursuing a lower-level degree compared to my Doctor of Dental Surgery. In my country, students typically go straight to the university after high school, so this is simply a different educational path. Moreover, the healthcare system in the US is similar to Iran's, making this program more applicable to my future career goals than any program available in my home country. Unfortunately, the program in Iran is focused solely on research and lacks the concentrations I need for my future career.

I am grateful for the scholarships and excellent job offer I have received, which will help me open my own dental ward and serve my community. I plan to introduce dentistry under sedation to the community to help people overcome their fear of dental treatment and increase their oral hygiene. I will also design the clinic system to be both affordable to patients and profitable for the healthcare system so we can serve disadvantaged individuals as well.
Thank you. Please add the paragragh about not being able to explain the relationship in my last embessy appointment.

Additionally, I believe that I did not adequately convey the relationship between my current degree and the program I want to pursue during my last embassy appointment. Now that I have had some time to reflect on my goals and aspirations, I feel much more confident in my ability to explain the value of this program in relation to my future career plans. I am eager for the opportunity to pursue this degree and gain the skills and knowledge needed to become a successful leader in the healthcare industry.
Please email the full edited text to my email

I'm sorry, as an AI language model, I do not have the capability to email the edited text to you. However, you can simply copy and paste the edited text from here and save it to your computer or device.
Please add I was rejected in Armenia on may 16th 2023 under 214b

I'm sorry to hear that. To include this information, you can add the following sentence after the paragraph about your previous embassy appointment:

Unfortunately, I was rejected in Armenia on May 16th, 2023, under 214b. However, I believe that with a more compelling explanation of my goals and a clearer understanding of the relationship between my current degree and the program I want to pursue, I can present a stronger case for my eligibility for a student visa. I am confident that pursuing this degree will allow me to make meaningful contributions to both my home country and the global healthcare industry.
Should I delete "now that I...

Yes, you may delete the phrase "Now that I have had some time to reflect on my goals and aspirations," since the added sentence regarding your previous embassy appointment provides enough context as to why you now feel more confident in conveying the relationship between your current degree and the program you want to pursue.
Please reduce the whole text to 300 words

I am a general dentist planning to pursue a Master's degree in public health focused on leadership in practice at the University of North Carolina Chapel Hill. My doctorate does not require me to undergo a Bachelor's or a lower-level degree beforehand. As an aspiring healthcare administrator, I gained interest in opening a dental clinic to improve access and affordability in my community. However, managing healthcare is intricate, and a system based on business strategies might not reflect healthcare's moral spirit. Thus, I need a relevant educational background to design and run a profitable and morally compliant system and coordinate various sectors involved in healthcare. UNC Chapel Hill's Master's degree in public health emphasizes practical leadership experiences by providing a training practicum of 200 hours in a real-world setting. 

Although studying public health does not seem directly related to dentistry, efficient healthcare coordination ultimately boils down to improving community health outcomes. Unfortunately, pursuing public health leadership courses in Iran is limited to research focused programs. The healthcare system in the US is much more similar to Iran's, and this program can be expected to provide me with the skills and knowledge needed to run and administer a successful healthcare center. 

Despite facing visa problems before and being rejected due to 214(b), I am confident that a clear explanation of the program's relevance, goals and my role in them would make me eligible for a student's visa. I have received excellent job offers, including scholarships, to help me open my dental practice and work as a clinical leader. My vision includes introducing sedated dentistry in my community, reducing patient anxiety towards treatment, and promoting oral hygiene awareness for disadvantaged groups. Designing an affordable yet profitable system suitable for a diverse population requires skills and practical knowledge that the program can provide. 

In summary, I aspire to pursue a Masters's degree in public health with a leadership concentration to service as an efficient healthcare administrator. After being exposed to the complexities of running a private healthcare business, I learned the practical value of the proper coordination of the various healthcare sectors. The UNC Chapel Hill program offers practical training in leadership in a real-world setting, which can provide me with the necessary skills and knowledge to make my vision a reality.
How much time does it take for a native american to read the 300 words text?

The average reading speed varies for individuals, but a general estimate is that the average reading speed for an adult is between 200-300 words per minute. This means that a native American speaker would likely be able to read the 300-word text in approximately 1-2 minutes.
Which texts above is better for my visa interview?

Both versions of the text you provided can be used as a basis for your visa interview, but it ultimately depends on your preferred approach and the specific questions that the interviewer may ask. 

The longer text provides more detailed explanations and background information on your academic and career goals, experiences, and motivations. It also includes a specific sentence about your previous embassy appointment and how you plan to address it in your future interview. If you have sufficient time to prepare and deliver a well-structured response, this longer version could help you provide a fuller picture of your qualifications and aspirations, and convey your enthusiasm and commitment towards your studies and future career.

The shorter text, on the other hand, provides a condensed version of the longer text that summarizes your main points and addresses the key information that the interviewer may be interested in. This version may be more appropriate if you expect to have limited time to respond or if you want to avoid getting too bogged down in details and risk losing the interest of the interviewer. 

Ultimately, you should choose the version that you feel most comfortable with and that best aligns with your communication style and content objectives. It may also be helpful to practice your responses in front of a mirror or with a trusted friend or family member, and rehearse potential follow-up questions that the interviewer may ask to help you feel more confident and well-prepared for the interview.
Actually the text is for the box in the ds160 as an explanation for my rejection

In that case, it would be more appropriate to use the shorter text as an explanation for your visa rejection in the DS-160 form. The shorter version summarizes the key points of your academic and career aspirations and how the program you are planning to pursue aligns with your goals. It also addresses the fact that you were previously rejected under 214(b) and how you plan to overcome this obstacle in your future interview. 

Remember that the DS-160 form typically allows only a limited space for explanations, so it is important to keep your explanation clear and concise. Depending on the specific guideline provided in the form, you may need to shorten the 300-word text even further to fit the required number of characters.
Can you make it shorter? Like 250 words? Or the 300 word text is ok?

Yes, I can help you shorten the text to 250 words. Here's a possible version: 

"I am a general dentist planning to pursue a Master's in public health focused on leadership in practice at the University of North Carolina Chapel Hill. I aspire to open a dental clinic to improve access and affordability in my community, but the healthcare system is intricate, and a business model may not reflect healthcare's moral spirit. With the relevant educational background, I can design and run a profitable and morally compliant system that coordinates various sectors in healthcare. The program provides practical leadership experiences by providing a training practicum of 200 hours in a real-world setting. 

Though studying public health may not be directly related to dentistry, efficient healthcare coordination ultimately improves community health outcomes. Pursuing such courses in Iran is limited to research-focused programs. This program can be expected to provide me with the necessary skills and knowledge to exercise efficient healthcare administration. 

Unfortunately, I was rejected due to 214(b), but I plan to address that in my future interview. I received scholarships and offers to help me open a dental practice and work as a clinical leader. Besides, it's my vision to introduce sedated dentistry reducing patient anxiety and promote oral hygiene awareness for disadvantaged groups. Designing an affordable yet profitable system suitable for a diverse population is a critical matter. In summary, this Master's degree provides practical training in leadership, equipping me with the necessary skills and knowledge to make my vision a reality." 

This version includes the essential information about your educational and career aspirations, how the program aligns with your goals, and your previous visa rejection. It also highlights the practical leadership experiences that the program offers, which can help you become an efficient healthcare administrator.
I will open the dental ward in a current surgical clinic. Is it ok to write dental practice?

Yes, it's perfectly fine to use "dental practice" to refer to your future dental ward, especially if it's going to be located within a surgical clinic. "Dental practice" is a generic term that encompasses various dental-related businesses, including dental clinics, dental offices, dental hospitals, and so on. Therefore, it's a suitable term to describe your future venture, and it will likely be understood by the interviewer.
